title: Selliguea albidopaleata
taxonomy: Selliguea albidopaleata is classified as a species of fern in the family Polypodiaceae. It belongs to the genus Selliguea, which is a group of epipetric ferns. This plant is native to the tropical regions of Asia and is characterized by its pale to white-colored rachides. Selliguea albidopaleata is a part of the order Polypodiales and is further classified as a member of the class Polypodiopsida. Its classification is also reflected in the International Plant Names Index, which lists it as a recognized species.
uses: Selliguea albidopaleata is a species of fern known to have various medicinal properties. Traditionally, it has been used in the treatment of fever, cough, and respiratory issues. The plant's extracts have been reported to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its medicinal uses. In folk medicine, Selliguea albidopaleata is also used to treat digestive problems and skin conditions. However, further research is required to confirm the efficacy and safety of its medicinal uses.

intro: Selliguea albidopaleata, also known as Selaginella albidopaleata, is a species of plant in the family Selaginellaceae, commonly referred to as a spikemoss or a resprouter. It has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Southeast Asia, to treat various health conditions, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its potent anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The bioactive constituents of Selliguea albidopaleata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include tea, infusions, and decoctions, which are used to treat various health conditions, although its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb require further scientific investigation.
